title: Monsoon Marinated Ethiopian Lamb Dabo
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish featuring lamb marinated in a mixture of spices and herbs, then char-grilled to perfection, served with a side of fermented glassy-jelly sauce. This low-carb friendly dish is perfect for dinner
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together lemon juice, garlic, ginger, berbere spice, and cinnamon stick to make the marinade
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and mix well to coat, cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight
Preheat a char-grill or grill pan to medium-high heat
Remove the lamb from the marinade and cook for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked to desired level of doneness
Transfer the lamb to a plate and let rest for 5 minutes before slicing
Meanwhile, prepare the fermented glassy-jelly sauce by mixing together 1 cup of plain yogurt, 1/2 cup of chopped fresh cilantro, and 1 tablespoon of lemon juice
Serve the sliced lamb with the fermented glassy-jelly sauce and a side of roasted vegetables
Garnish with additional cilantro and cinnamon stick if desired
Refrigerate any leftovers for up to 3 days
